events.title: South Korean Vessel Attacked in Strait of Hormuz
events.desc: A South Korean vessel was targeted in the Strait of Hormuz, prompting a call from President Trump for Seoul to join U.S.-led operations to secure the waterway. The South Korean government is reportedly reviewing the request, highlighting the broader challenge of securing international cooperation amid the ongoing maritime crisis.

events.title: Indian BrahMos Missile Strikes Hit Pakistani Air Bases
events.desc: In May 2025, Indian BrahMos missile strikes targeted Pakistani air bases, hitting runways and parked aircraft. Islamabad opted not to retaliate with its long-range systems, including the dual-capable Babur cruise missile, to avoid signaling nuclear escalation, indicating a new logic of limited war in South Asia.

events.title: Iranian Drone Attacks on Gulf Arab States
events.desc: Drones, improved with Russian technology and battlefield testing, have been hitting Gulf Arab states daily since February. These attacks underscore the growing integration of adversarial capabilities and highlight a new dynamic where even U.S. partners are seeking assistance from countries like Ukraine to counter these threats.

events.title: Iran's Police Infrastructure Targeted
events.desc: In the first three weeks of the U.S. and Israeli war against Iran, at least 80 police stations or infrastructure belonging to law enforcement and the Basij paramilitary group have been damaged or destroyed. This campaign aims to degrade the Iranian regime's 'repressive capacity'.

events.title: Drone Strike on UAE-Owned Tanker Off Oman
events.desc: A unit of the Abu Dhabi National Oil Company reported a small fuel leak from one of its tankers that was struck by Iranian drones last week off the coast of Oman. This incident highlights the ecological risks associated with the ongoing conflict and the perilous nature of navigating the Strait of Hormuz.

events.title: Israeli Drone Strikes Kill Civilians in Lebanon
events.desc: At least eight people, including two children, were killed in three Israeli drone strikes targeting cars on a highway in Jiyeh, south of Beirut. This attack escalates tensions in the region, with ongoing exchanges of fire between Israel and Hezbollah.

events.title: UN Resolution Calls for Freedom of Navigation in Strait of Hormuz
events.desc: The United Nations has passed a resolution calling for freedom of navigation in the Strait of Hormuz. This diplomatic move aims to address the ongoing maritime crisis and ensure the unimpeded flow of vital shipping through the critical waterway.

events.title: Hungarian Government Passwords Exposed Online
events.desc: Nearly 800 Hungarian government email addresses and their associated passwords have been found circulating online. The breach affects 12 out of 13 ministries, exposing potential vulnerabilities in sensitive government data security.

events.title: US and China Agree on Opposing Hormuz Tolls
events.desc: Senior U.S. and Chinese officials have agreed that no country should be allowed to impose shipping tolls in the Strait of Hormuz. This agreement signals a potential common ground between the two powers in pressuring Iran over its control of the vital waterway, ahead of a key summit.

events.title: Britain to Legislate Against Hostile State Proxies
events.desc: The UK government will introduce legislation to combat threats from proxies of hostile states. This move is a response to increased activity within Britain and a rise in antisemitic attacks, aiming to strengthen the nation's ability to deal with such threats.

events.title: Israel Escalates Gaza Attacks Post-Iran Truce
events.desc: Israel has increased its attacks in Gaza since pausing joint bombing operations with the U.S. in Iran. The Gaza Health Ministry reports a 20% increase in Palestinian deaths compared to the period when Israeli forces were conducting strikes in Iran, with the Israeli military asserting that Hamas is rearming.

events.title: Lebanon Reports 39 Deaths in Israeli Strikes
events.desc: Lebanon's health ministry stated that 39 people were killed in recent Israeli strikes. This follows continued exchanges of fire between Israel and Hezbollah, despite a recently announced ceasefire deal, indicating a fragile security situation.

events.title: Iran Demands Guarantees for World Cup Participation
events.desc: The Iranian Football Federation (FFIRI) has presented FIFA with 10 conditions for participation in the World Cup. This demand comes amid geopolitical tensions and suggests Iran is seeking assurances that its participation will not be politically compromised.

events.title: Israeli Law Allows Death Penalty for 7 October Attackers
events.desc: Israel has passed a law permitting the death penalty and public trials for individuals involved in the Hamas-led attacks of October 7. This legislative move reflects a hardening stance and aims to provide severe legal consequences for those deemed responsible.

events.title: EU Sanctions Israeli Settlers Over West Bank Violence
events.desc: The European Union has agreed to impose sanctions on Israeli settlers responsible for violence in the West Bank. The EU's foreign policy chief stated that 'extremism and violence carry consequences,' a move Israel has described as 'arbitrary.'
